Primary Function: Pumps blood throughout body
Key Facts: Beats ~100000 times/day; ~5L blood pumped per minute at rest

Primary Function: Largest artery; carries oxygenated blood from heart
Key Facts: ~30 cm long; 2.5–3.5 cm diameter

Primary Function: Largest veins; return deoxygenated blood to heart
Key Facts: Superior and inferior vena cava

Primary Function: Gas exchange (O2 in / CO2 out)
Key Facts: Right has 3 lobes; left has 2 lobes; total surface area ~70 m²

Primary Function: Main muscle of breathing; separates thorax from abdomen
Key Facts: Contracts during inhalation

Primary Function: Airways passage from larynx to lungs
Key Facts: ~12 cm long; reinforced by C-shaped cartilage rings

Primary Function: Detoxification / bile production / protein synthesis / glycogen storage
Key Facts: Largest internal organ; only organ that can fully regenerate

Primary Function: Nutrient absorption
Key Facts: ~6–7 m long; lined with villi for absorption

Primary Function: Water absorption / waste formation
Key Facts: ~1.5 m long; houses most gut microbiome

Primary Function: Digestion of food; acid production
Key Facts: Holds ~1–1.5L food; secretes HCl and pepsin

Primary Function: Produces insulin / glucagon / digestive enzymes
Key Facts: Both endocrine and exocrine gland

Primary Function: Stores and concentrates bile
Key Facts: Bile helps digest fats; can form gallstones

Primary Function: Transports food from mouth to stomach
Key Facts: ~25 cm long; uses peristalsis

Primary Function: Filters blood; produces urine; regulates electrolytes
Key Facts: Filter ~180L blood per day; 1 million nephrons each

Primary Function: Stores urine
Key Facts: Holds ~300–500 mL; signals urge at ~150 mL

Primary Function: Carries urine from kidneys to bladder
Key Facts: ~25–30 cm long each

Primary Function: Regulates metabolism / growth / energy
Key Facts: Produces T3 and T4; requires iodine

Primary Function: Produces adrenaline / cortisol / aldosterone
Key Facts: Two parts: cortex (steroid hormones) and medulla (adrenaline)

Primary Function: Master gland; controls other hormones
Key Facts: Size of a pea; produces GH / TSH / FSH / LH etc.

Primary Function: Links nervous system to endocrine system; temperature regulation
Key Facts: Controls pituitary; involved in hunger / thirst / sleep

Primary Function: Produces melatonin; regulates sleep-wake cycle
Key Facts: Size of a grain of rice

Primary Function: Produce sperm and testosterone
Key Facts: External for lower temperature needed for sperm

Primary Function: Produce eggs / oestrogen / progesterone
Key Facts: Contain ~1–2 million follicles at birth

Primary Function: Houses and nourishes developing foetus
Key Facts: Expands to ~1100g during pregnancy

Primary Function: Control centre for body; cognition / emotion / motor control
Key Facts: ~86 billion neurons; 2% of body weight; uses 20% of energy

Primary Function: Transmits signals between brain and body; reflex arcs
Key Facts: ~45 cm long; 31 pairs of spinal nerves

Primary Function: Coordinates movement / balance / fine motor skills
Key Facts: Contains ~50% of all neurons in the brain

Primary Function: Vision; detects light and converts to neural signals
Key Facts: Can distinguish ~10 million colours

Primary Function: Hearing and balance
Key Facts: Cochlea converts sound waves to electrical signals

Primary Function: Smell; also filters and warms air
Key Facts: ~400 types of olfactory receptors

Primary Function: Taste / speech / swallowing
Key Facts: ~10000 taste buds; replaced every 10–14 days

Primary Function: Filters blood / destroys old RBCs / immune response
Key Facts: Can be removed; other organs take over function

Primary Function: Matures T-lymphocytes (T-cells)
Key Facts: Most active in childhood; shrinks after puberty

Primary Function: Filter lymph fluid; house immune cells
Key Facts: ~600–700 nodes in human body

Primary Function: Barrier / temperature regulation / sensation / vitamin D synthesis
Key Facts: Largest organ by area (~2 m²); shed completely every ~2–4 weeks

Primary Function: Movement / posture / heat generation
Key Facts: ~640 muscles in the human body; ~40% of body weight

Primary Function: Support / protection / blood cell production / mineral storage
Key Facts: Strongest bone: femur; smallest: stapes in ear

Primary Function: Produces red and white blood cells
Key Facts: Yellow marrow stores fat; red marrow produces blood cells
